zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de la Mare於大約1457年出生在英國時，她的父親Sir Thomas de la Mare38歲，母親Lady Elizabeth Fynderne29歲。她於大約1480年與Robert Leynham結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1499年12月5日在英國波克夏迪德馬緒享年43歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de la Mare于大约1457年出生在英国时，她的父亲Sir Thomas de la Mare38岁，母亲Lady Elizabeth Fynderne29岁。她于大约1480年与Robert Leynham结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1499年12月5日在英国波克夏迪德馬緒享年43岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Mare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Americo, Cesareo, Fernando, Ines, Javier, Jose, Marcos.  Portuguese Henrique.  Italian Antonio, Gino, Olindo, Silvio, Vito.  Dutch Andries.
Italian: topographic name for someone living by the sea, from mare ‘sea’.
English: variant of   Mear  .

variants.name: Marucci
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Antonio, Silvio, Angelo, Carmela, Domenic, Donato, Egidio, Guido, Licinio, Lorenzo, Luciano.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Maruccio, a pet form of Mare or Mari .

variants.name: Marelli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Giuseppe, Angelo, Dante.
Italian:
patronymic or plural form of a diminutive of   Mare  .

variants.name: Mear
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Franz, Helmut, Wilhelm, Erwin, Gerhard, Heinz, Ilse, Lorenz, Wolfgang.

English (Staffordshire): post-medieval form of Mear, which is either a topographic name, from Middle English mere ‘pond, pool’ (Old English mere) or Middle English mere ‘boundary’ (Old English (ge)mǣre), or a habitational name from one or more of the many places with names derived from these terms, such as Mere (Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Wiltshire), Meir in Caverswall (Staffordshire), and Meare (Somerset).
English: occupational name for a physician, from Old French mire, Middle English mire, meir, meyre, originally synonymous with Myer .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de la Mare was born about 1457, in England, her father, Sir Thomas de la Mare, was 38 and her mother, Lady Elizabeth Fynderne, was 29. She married Robert Leynham about 1480. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 5 December 1499, in Tidmarsh, Berkshire, England, at the age of 43.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de la Mare est née environ 1457, à Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas de la Mare, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Lady Elizabeth Fynderne, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé Robert Leynham environ 1480. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 5 décembre 1499, à Tidmarsh, Berkshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 43 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de la Mareが約1457年にイギリスで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas de la Mareは38歳，母Lady Elizabeth Fynderneは29歳であった。約1480年にRobert Leynhamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1499年12月5日にイギリスバークシュアイアティドマーッシュで43歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de la Mare(이)가 대략 1457년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas de la Mare(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Elizabeth Fynderne(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 대략 1480년 Robert Leynham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1499년 12월 5일 영국 버크실르 티드마시에서 43세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de la Mare родилась около 1457 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas de la Mare было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Elizabeth Fynderne, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Leynham около 1480 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 5 декабря 1499 г. в местности тидмарш, биркшир, Англия в возрасте 43 лет.
